section.title: Introduction and Overview
section.p: riscos-allocate converts Allocate request files to and from a textual YAML description. The tool reads the native Allocate binary request format with filetype &FB0, produces a human-readable UTF-8 description, and can recreate a binary request from that description.

The textual format is intended for inspection, editing, and source control. It preserves the developer details, the ordered list of registrations, and the filetype attachments required by filetype requests. Attachments may either be embedded inline in the YAML document or detached into sibling files referenced from the YAML.

subsection.title: Tool Behaviour
subsection.p: Without conversion switches, the tool displays a human-readable summary of the binary Allocate request. With --extract it writes a YAML description, and with --create it reads the YAML description and writes a native Allocate request file.

The tool emits warnings to the standard error stream when the YAML description is structurally malformed, contains unknown fields, omits expected fields, uses values of the wrong type, refers to missing detached files, or contains text that cannot be represented in Latin-1 without replacement.

subsection.title: Input and Output Selection
subsection.p:
The positional file argument is interpreted according to the selected operation:
For display and extraction it names a native Allocate request file.
For creation it may name either a YAML file or a directory containing Allocation.yaml.

When extracting to a detached directory with --extract-files, the tool writes Allocation.yaml and any detached attachment files into the named directory. When creating from a detached description, the tool resolves those attachment filenames relative to the YAML file or the directory containing it.

subsection.title: Text Encoding
subsection.p: Developer-detail fields and fixed text fields in the binary Allocate format are stored as fixed-width Latin-1 byte arrays. Text attachments within filetype registrations are also treated as Latin-1. The YAML description is written as UTF-8, so binary Latin-1 text is decoded to Unicode when extracting and is encoded back to Latin-1 when creating a request file.
If a YAML value contains characters outside Latin-1, the tool warns and replaces each unrepresentable character with ? during creation.

subsection.title: Top-Level YAML Structure
subsection.p: The YAML document is a mapping with the following top-level keys:

definition-table.definition: Version of the YAML description schema. The current tool writes 1.
Allocate request-file version. The current Allocate file format uses 1.00, corresponding to binary value 100.
Sequence of registration objects in the same order as the binary request file.

subsection.p: Empty optional scalar fields are written as YAML null.

subsection.title: Developer Mapping
subsection.p: The Developer mapping contains these keys:

subsection.title: Registration Sequence
subsection.p: Each entry in Registrations is a mapping containing exactly one registration type name and its field mapping. The type names written by the tool are:

definition-table.definition: ApplicationName, Name, DoubleClicking, and attachment fields.

subsection.p: For compatibility, the creator also accepts space-separated and title-cased variants of these names, such as SWIChunk and SWI chunk.

subsection.title: Filetype Attachments
subsection.p:
Filetype registrations carry three attachments: a sprite file, a product description, and a format description. In inline YAML output:
Sprite contains the sprite data encoded as base64 text.
ProductDescription contains the product description as decoded text.
FormatDescription contains the format description as decoded text.
When --extract-files is used, these inline fields are replaced by filename references:

definition-table.definition: Sprite attachment, normally written with host suffix ,ff9.
Product description attachment, written as a text file.
Format description attachment, written as a text file.

subsection.p: If both an inline field and the corresponding detached-file field are present when creating a request, the detached file takes precedence and a warning is emitted.

command-definition.name: riscos-allocate
command-definition.description: Display, extract, or create Allocate request files
command-definition.parameter: Write the YAML description to standard output or to the file named by -o.
Read a YAML description from file and create a binary Allocate request. The input may be either a YAML file or a directory containing Allocation.yaml.
Write a detached YAML export into the named directory. The directory receives Allocation.yaml and any detached attachment files.
Write extracted YAML or created binary output to the named file instead of standard output.
Input file or directory, interpreted according to the selected mode.

use.p: --extract and --create are mutually exclusive. --extract-files implies --extract and cannot be combined with -o.
In create mode, binary output is written to standard output when -o is omitted. In extract mode, YAML is written to standard output when -o is omitted.

subsection.title: Inline YAML Export
subsection.p: The following abbreviated example shows the structure of an inline YAML description:

p.extended-example: SchemaVersion: 1 FormatVersion: 1.00 Developer: Name: Justin Fletcher Company: Gerph Email: gerph@gerph.org Registrations: - Filetype: ApplicationName: null Name: Zip DoubleClicking: true Sprite: | U1BSSVRFRklMRQ== ProductDescription: | Product line 1 Product line 2 - Reservation: Type: ModName Text: WebColours

subsection.title: Detached YAML Export
subsection.p: When attachments are detached, the YAML refers to sibling files instead of embedding their content:

p.extended-example: SchemaVersion: 1 FormatVersion: 1.00 Developer: Name: Justin Fletcher Registrations: - Filetype: ApplicationName: null Name: Zip DoubleClicking: true SpriteFile: 1-Filetype-Sprite,ff9 ProductDescriptionFile: 1-Filetype-ProductDescription.txt FormatDescriptionFile: 1-Filetype-FormatDescription.txt

revision.author: Charles (Gerph)
revision.title: Initial version
revision.change: Documented the riscos-allocate conversion tool, its command-line interface, and the YAML description format used to extract and recreate Allocate request files.
